title: Pan Seared Italian Pork Chops Gratin
description: A hearty Italian dish featuring pan seared pork chops, confit tomato, and a glassy jelly sauce, served in a cozy ramekin.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork chops with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat and sear the pork chops for 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the pork chops to a plate and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the chopped onion to the skillet, cooking until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the white wine and chicken broth to the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Bring the liquid to a simmer and cook until reduced by half.
Stir in the confit tomato and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Transfer the pork chops to a ramekin and spoon the tomato sauce over the top.
Sprinkle the shaved grana padano cheese over the top of the pork chops and bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes.
